steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must explain why finding Ediacaran organisms requires looking at Mistaken Point rather than at the Asian hominin sites. The key logical structure: Ediacaran organisms are older than hominins, so scientists must go to a site whose geological formation is from an earlier time period. The answer needs to link the age of the formation at Mistaken Point to the age of the organisms found there.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "North America has sites with hominins but not sites with early multicellular organisms.": contradicts the passage, which cites Mistaken Point (in North America) as a place to find early multicellular organisms. ✗
  • "early multicellular organisms are found at more sites around the world than hominins.": the passage makes no comparison of how many sites contain each type of organism. ✗
  • "that formation is from an earlier time than the formation with the hominins.": directly and logically explains why Mistaken Point yields older specimens — its geological formation predates the Asian hominin formation. ✓
  • "the hominins at Mistaken Point are better preserved than the hominins at the Minatogawa Limestone Quarry.": Mistaken Point contains Ediacaran organisms, not hominins; this choice misidentifies what is found at each site. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
